message:
feat(rpc): port bindresvport6 IPv6 reserved-port bind helper (bd-ghm72)

Adds glibc's `int bindresvport6(int sd, struct sockaddr_in6 *sin6)`
— the IPv6 companion to the existing bindresvport. Binds the
socket to a privileged IPv6 port (512..1023) by trying ports in
a process-id-rotated order until one succeeds. If `sin6` is NULL,
an in6addr_any sockaddr_in6 is synthesized internally. Returns 0
on success, -1 on failure with errno set (EADDRINUSE if no
reserved port is available).

Implementation: mirrors the existing bindresvport flow in
rpc_abi but uses sockaddr_in6 + AF_INET6 throughout. Same
process-id-rotated start offset (600..1023) and same
EADDRINUSE-vs-other-error fast-fail behavior on the first non-
in-use error.

Tests: 3 abi integration tests covering: (a) invalid socket
returns -1; (b) successful bind on a fresh UDP/IPv6 socket
puts the port within the reserved range, verified via
getsockname (treated as inconclusive when CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE
is unavailable, e.g. when tests run as non-root); (c) NULL
sockaddr argument works using internal storage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-geoa","title":"resolv: getaddrinfo_a zero-item and null-request-list degenerate calls should succeed","description":"Host glibc returns success with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate getaddrinfo_a calls such as nitems=0 (even with a NULL list) and positive-length request arrays whose entries are all NULL. FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM/ENOSYS. Restore those no-op success semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:27:52.529653096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.157717821Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:30:33.155545909Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity getaddrinfo_a success semantics for zero-item and all-NULL synchronous-degenerate request sets, with focused ABI regression coverage verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gh8m4","title":"syscall: sched priority bounds for SCHED_DEADLINE should be zero exactly","description":"Host glibc returns min=0 and max=0 for sched_get_priority_{min,max}(SCHED_DEADLINE). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the Linux deadline-policy bounds contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:33:47.405198256Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.365913964Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:42:33.363277112Z","close_reason":"Added sched_priority_bounds_sched_deadline_are_zero asserting min=max=0 host-parity for SCHED_DEADLINE (policy 6).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ghm72","title":"PORT: bindresvport6 (glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper)","description":"Port the glibc IPv6 reserved-port bind helper `int bindresvport6(int sd, struct sockaddr_in6 *sin6)`. Companion to the existing IPv4 bindresvport — binds the socket to a privileged IPv6 port (512..1023) by trying ports in a process-id-rotated order until one binds successfully. If `sin6` is NULL, an in6addr_any sockaddr_in6 is synthesized. Returns 0 on success, -1 on failure with errno set (EADDRINUSE if no reserved port is available). Implementation: mirror the existing bindresvport flow in rpc_abi but use sockaddr_in6 + AF_INET6. Tests: bind to a fresh socket succeeds and reports the port within the reserved range; supplying a NULL sockaddr works (function uses internal storage); EADDRINUSE-vs-other-error path is exercised when bind hits a real failure (e.g. closed-socket EBADF).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:12.585026069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:51:12.585026069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gii3","title":"raise(0) incorrectly rejects POSIX null signal","description":"Our frankenlibc_abi::signal_abi::raise() checks valid_signal(sig) which requires sig in 1..=64. POSIX specifies raise(0) must succeed as the 'null signal' (no-op sending, checks tgkill permissions only). Host glibc's raise(0) does tgkill(pid,tid,0) which succeeds. Impacts gsignal(0) via raise(). Fix: add explicit null-signal handling to signal_abi::raise() before valid_signal check, calling tgkill(pid,tid,0) directly. Discovered while expanding signal_ops conformance fixtures (bd-9n4k).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T17:45:39.193198411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.339368276Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T17:56:13.337394740Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX null-signal raise/gsignal semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gijn2","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] inet_abi entry points walk unbounded user-supplied pointers (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 0c0b59f1).

Four ABI entry points (inet_pton, inet_aton, inet_addr, if_nametoindex) dereferenced caller-supplied *const c_char via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated argument walked arbitrary process memory until hitting an accidental zero byte — same info-leak / segfault class as bd-z4k96 (locale), cab4670a (iconv), 6f1e3036 (dlfcn).

Reachability: every program that parses an IP address from caller-controlled text (config readers, network-protocol parsers, getaddrinfo fallbacks) hits inet_pton/inet_aton/inet_addr; if_nametoindex is reached by every routing/iface-options call.

Fix: add read_bounded_cstr (mirrors locale/iconv/dlfcn copies) and replace each unbounded CStr::from_ptr; on None return the function's standard invalid-input sentinel.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-gilq3","title":"[HIGH] bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test fails: 'could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so'","description":"## Symptom

`cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` fails:

```
thread 'fixture_gap_fill_gate_emits_valid_bd15n2_artifacts' panicked at
crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42:5:
gate script failed:
stdout=
stderr=    Finished `release` profile [optimized] target(s) in 0.06s
FAIL: could not locate libfrankenlibc_abi.so
```

## Diagnosis

The test at line 42 invokes a gate script (presumably under scripts/) that needs the built `libfrankenlibc_abi.so` cdylib. The script builds a release profile of frankenlibc-abi (that step succeeds in the above log), then fails to *find* the resulting .so at the path it expects. The stderr only shows the cargo 'Finished release' line, no locator stderr from the script — the script likely greps `target/release` for the .so but we're running under `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc` which the script doesn't respect.

## Fix direction

1. Read crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test.rs:42 and trace which gate script it spawns.
2. That script likely hardcodes `target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`; change it to honour `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (or at least look at `/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so` when set) or fall back to `cargo metadata --format-version=1 | jq -r .target_directory`/`release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so`.

## Blast radius

Fails even in-isolation — test is always broken under a non-default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`. Blocks the bd15n2 fixture-gap-fill gate from being exercised in any CI environment that redirects target_dir (rch, sccache, CI caches, etc.).

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test` — reproduces reliably with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc`.
- Expected to pass under default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` (unset → defaults to `target/`).
","notes":"Fixed by cc-libc in the same push.

Root cause: scripts/bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_run.sh maintained a hardcoded LIB_CANDIDATES array of three paths (target/release, target/debug, /data/tmp/cargo-target/release) and never considered a caller-supplied CARGO_TARGET_DIR. Under rch (which redirects to /tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc) or any other non-default target-dir setup, the script's rebuild step put the .so somewhere the candidate list never looked.

Fix: append "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" and "${CARGO_TARGET_DIR}/debug/libfrankenlibc_abi.so" to LIB_CANDIDATES when the env var is set, matching the pattern that scripts/e2e_suite.sh already uses correctly.

Extended the same fix to scripts/c_fixture_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh, which had the identical bug — those are called by sibling gates that would have tripped next.

Also improved the error message to print the searched-path list when the library can't be located, so future breakages are diagnosable at a glance.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test bd15n2_fixture_gap_fill_artifacts_test -> 1 passed, 0 failed, 28.96s (worker vmi1152480).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-libc","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:18:06.994113107Z","created_by":"cc-libc","updated_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T13:46:11.806847Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cdylib","full-review","harness","test-infra"]}
@@ -1181,7 +1182,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-voxzi","title":"PORT: strnstr (BSD bounded substring search)","description":"BSD strnstr(haystack, needle, n) — like strstr but searches at most n bytes of haystack, returning a pointer to the first occurrence of needle (still NUL-terminated) or NULL if not found. Used by many BSD ports; without it programs that depend on it will not link against frankenlibc. Currently missing from frankenlibc-abi/string_abi.rs. Ship core::string::str::strnstr (returning Option<usize>) plus an abi shim that handles NULL pointer and casts to char*.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T15:14:29.383398661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.591905897Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T15:18:35.590011542Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::string::str::strnstr + string_abi::strnstr; 12+10 tests; bound-respecting via core_strnlen; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
 {"id":"bd-vqevs","title":"PORT: sys_signame (BSD short signal-name array)","description":"Port the BSD `const char *const sys_signame[NSIG]` array — short uppercase signal names without the "SIG" prefix, indexed by signal number. Entries 1..31 are "HUP", "INT", "QUIT", "ILL", "TRAP", "ABRT", "BUS", "FPE", "KILL", "USR1", "SEGV", "USR2", "PIPE", "ALRM", "TERM", "STKFLT", "CHLD", "CONT", "STOP", "TSTP", "TTIN", "TTOU", "URG", "XCPU", "XFSZ", "VTALRM", "PROF", "WINCH", "IO", "PWR", "SYS"; entry 0 is empty; entries 32..64 are realtime placeholders. NetBSD/FreeBSD callers expect this symbol; some Linux ports (libbsd) provide it. Implementation: same repr(transparent) SysSigList wrapper pattern as the recently-shipped sys_siglist, but holding the abbreviated name strings instead of the descriptions. Tests verify length, well-known indices, and matching shape against sigabbrev_np.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:45:13.092404101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:48:48.457995157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:48:48.455973509Z","close_reason":"shipped: sys_signame[NSIG] BSD short signal-name array (HUP/INT/KILL/...) reusing the SysSigList repr(transparent) wrapper + 5 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-vwrz8","title":"PORT: __pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait + __pthread_attr_init/destroy (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread condvar lifecycle + attr lifecycle: __pthread_cond_init, __pthread_cond_destroy, __pthread_cond_timedwait, __pthread_attr_init, __pthread_attr_destroy. These complete the underscored-alias coverage for the condvar and thread-attribute families that libstdc++ thread / std::condition_variable machinery links against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait and pthread_attr_init/destroy. Tests verify each alias works the same as its public counterpart on a fresh init / destroy round trip and a timed-wait that returns ETIMEDOUT.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:42:32.220396231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:42:32.220396231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-vwrz8","title":"PORT: __pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait + __pthread_attr_init/destroy (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread condvar lifecycle + attr lifecycle: __pthread_cond_init, __pthread_cond_destroy, __pthread_cond_timedwait, __pthread_attr_init, __pthread_attr_destroy. These complete the underscored-alias coverage for the condvar and thread-attribute families that libstdc++ thread / std::condition_variable machinery links against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait and pthread_attr_init/destroy. Tests verify each alias works the same as its public counterpart on a fresh init / destroy round trip and a timed-wait that returns ETIMEDOUT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:42:32.220396231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:46:30.519993981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:46:30.517740001Z","close_reason":"shipped: __pthread_cond_init/destroy/timedwait + __pthread_attr_init/destroy glibc reserved-namespace aliases delegating to existing public-name impls + 4 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vyvv","title":"string: execute string_memory_full.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/string_memory_full_conformance_test.rs currently stops at in-process execute_fixture_case coverage.
